evidence: plugin.json has no commands array; 10 command files found under commands/
description: Plugin manifest declares no commands array; plugin installs without registering any /notion:* slash commands
suggested_fix: Add 'commands' array listing the 10 command paths relative to plugin root (e.g. 'commands/find.md')

evidence: plugin.json has no skills array; 4 SKILL.md files found under skills/notion/
description: Plugin manifest declares no skills array; plugin installs without loading any Notion workspace skills
suggested_fix: Add 'skills' array listing paths to the 4 Notion skill directories

evidence: 'type':'http','url':'https://mcp.notion.com/mcp' — external HTTP transport confirmed in .mcp.json
description: MCP server uses external HTTP transport to mcp.notion.com; Notion workspace content traverses Notion's cloud API endpoint
suggested_fix: Document in README that workspace data traverses Notion's MCP API endpoint and link to Notion's data handling policy

description: MCP server registered as 'notion' in .mcp.json but called 'notionApi' in commands and referenced with 'Notion:' prefix in SKILL.md tool calls — three names for one server
suggested_fix: Standardize the server name; if the Notion MCP server self-identifies as 'Notion', update .mcp.json key from 'notion' to 'Notion' and update command prose accordingly

evidence: frontmatter has no allowed-tools field
description: Command declares no allowed-tools; any tool may be invoked without declaration
suggested_fix: Add 'allowed-tools: mcp__notion__*' (or the applicable tool glob) to frontmatter

evidence: Step 1 parses $ARGUMENTS with no branch for empty input
description: Command has no handling for empty $ARGUMENTS; will attempt database resolution with no target specified
suggested_fix: Add an empty-input branch: 'If $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask the user to specify a database name or ID and the properties to set'

evidence: Step 1 parses $ARGUMENTS with no branch for empty input
description: Command has no handling for empty $ARGUMENTS; will attempt to parse page title with no input
suggested_fix: Add an empty-input branch: 'If $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask the user for the page title and optional parent'

evidence: Step 1 lists 'Task title (required)' but no empty-$ARGUMENTS guard
description: Command documents task title as required but provides no branch when $ARGUMENTS is entirely absent
suggested_fix: Add: 'If $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask the user for a task title before proceeding'

evidence: Step 1 parses $ARGUMENTS with no branch for empty input
description: Command has no handling for empty $ARGUMENTS; will attempt database query with no target
suggested_fix: Add: 'If $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask the user to specify a database name or ID'

evidence: Behavior block treats $ARGUMENTS as fuzzy search terms with no empty guard
description: Command has no handling for empty $ARGUMENTS; will run an empty-string Notion search
suggested_fix: Add: 'If $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask the user what to search for'

evidence: Behavior block interprets $ARGUMENTS as search query with no empty guard
description: Command has no handling for empty $ARGUMENTS; will run an empty-string workspace search
suggested_fix: Add: 'If $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask the user what to search for'

description: Notes handles invalid/inaccessible URL but not absent $ARGS; empty input goes unguarded
suggested_fix: Add: 'If $ARGS is empty, ask the user to provide a Notion task URL'

description: Notes handles invalid/inaccessible URL but not absent $ARGS; empty input goes unguarded
suggested_fix: Add: 'If $ARGS is empty, ask the user to provide a Notion task URL'

evidence: Behavior section uses bullet list; no numbered steps for the search-then-present-then-handle-empty flow
description: Multi-step command (search → present results → handle empty case) uses unordered bullets instead of numbered steps
suggested_fix: Replace Behavior bullet list with numbered steps: 1. Search, 2. Present results, 3. Handle empty case

evidence: Behavior section uses bullet list; no numbered steps for the search-then-summarize-then-handle-empty flow
description: Multi-step command (search → summarize results → handle empty case) uses unordered bullets instead of numbered steps
suggested_fix: Replace Behavior bullet list with numbered steps: 1. Search, 2. Summarize results, 3. Handle empty case

evidence: File has Task/Input/Sections/Formatting headers but no numbered execution steps at top level
description: Command has a multi-step execution flow (fetch changes, create Notion page, write each section) but provides no numbered process steps — only output structure sections
suggested_fix: Add a numbered execution workflow above the Sections list: 1. Identify code changes, 2. Create Notion page, 3. Write each section in order, 4. Return page URL

evidence: Two-path wizard uses ### headers with sub-bullets; no numbered top-level process steps
description: Setup wizard has a multi-step flow (choose path → execute path → verify access → confirm) but lacks numbered top-level steps
suggested_fix: Add numbered top-level steps wrapping the two option paths: 1. Determine setup path, 2. Complete setup, 3. Verify access, 4. Confirm readiness

evidence: 'sensible default structure' line 14; 'brief clarification question' line 13
description: 'sensible' and 'brief' are vague quantifiers that leave the scope of the default structure and the length of the clarification undefined
suggested_fix: Replace 'sensible default structure' with a concrete description (e.g., 'a two-section default: Overview and Notes'); replace 'brief' with a measurable bound (e.g., 'one-sentence')

evidence: 'appropriate Tasks database' line 17; 'confidently identified' line 27; 'concise clarification' line 27
description: 'appropriate', 'confidently', and 'concise' are vague quantifiers leaving selection criteria and question scope undefined
suggested_fix: Specify criteria: 'a database whose name contains Task or Todo'; replace 'confidently identified' with a concrete rule (e.g., 'exactly one match found'); replace 'concise' with a measurable bound

evidence: 'reasonable number of rows (e.g. 20-50)' line 16; 'compact table-like format' line 18
description: 'reasonable' and 'compact' are vague; the example range partially anchors the former but the latter is undefined
suggested_fix: Replace 'reasonable' with the concrete default (e.g., 'at most 25 rows by default'); replace 'compact table-like format' with an explicit format spec (e.g., 'markdown table with columns: Name, Status, Owner, Due')

evidence: 'best matches' line 19; 'better to show 5-10 very relevant results'
description: 'best matches' is undefined; the 5-10 range hint partially constrains it but 'best' still lacks a ranking criterion
suggested_fix: Replace 'best matches' with a concrete ranking rule (e.g., 'matches ranked by title similarity to $ARGUMENTS; top 10 by relevance score')

evidence: 'high-signal tools' line 13; 'short, scannable list' line 14
description: 'high-signal' and 'short' are undefined; leaves tool selection and result count ambiguous
suggested_fix: Specify tool priority order (e.g., 'prefer workspace search over database query for speed'); bound 'short' (e.g., 'at most 10 results')

evidence: 'short generic text description' line 22; 'keep it brief' line 28
description: 'short generic' and 'brief' leave Agent Status field length undefined; examples partly help but the rule remains vague
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete character limit (e.g., 'one emoji + 2-4 words, max 30 characters')

evidence: 'broadly explore surrounding code' line 16; 'use figures liberally' line 22; 'medium difficulty' in quiz section
description: 'broadly', 'liberally', and 'medium difficulty' are vague scope/frequency/difficulty terms with no measurable bound
suggested_fix: Replace 'broadly explore' with a concrete scope (e.g., 'read all files in the same directory as changed files'); replace 'liberally' with a floor (e.g., 'at least one diagram per major section'); define quiz difficulty criteria

evidence: 'ask just a few questions at a time' line 28; 'too many questions' line 28; 'briefly tell the user' line 34
description: 'a few', 'too many', and 'briefly' leave question batching and summary length undefined
suggested_fix: Replace 'a few' with a concrete bound (e.g., 'no more than 3 questions per message'); replace 'briefly' with a sentence limit (e.g., '2-3 sentences')

evidence: 'Be conversational and helpful' line 38
description: 'helpful' is a vague quality descriptor with no measurable criterion
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete behavior description (e.g., 'confirm each step before proceeding and offer to undo if the user changes their mind')

evidence: 'appropriate documentation format' step 2; 'relevant metadata' step 3; 'appropriate title' step 5; 'properly connected' line 108; 'periodically' line 180
description: Five or more distinct vague quantifiers across the skill; penalty capped at -20
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate' with explicit decision rules; replace 'relevant' with named field lists; replace 'periodically' with a time interval

evidence: 'relevant pages' appears 10+ times; 'recent updates' line 51; 'important meetings' line 187; 'Enrich thoughtfully' line 266
description: Four or more distinct vague quantifiers with 'relevant' appearing 10+ times; penalty capped at -20
suggested_fix: Define 'relevant' with explicit criteria (e.g., 'pages modified in the last 30 days matching any meeting attendee or topic keyword'); replace 'important' with a decision rule

evidence: 'relevant content' step 2; 'relevant sources' step 2; 'appropriate documentation template' step 4; 'recent' in Best Practices
description: Three or more distinct vague quantifiers with 'relevant' appearing 8+ times; penalty capped at -20
suggested_fix: Replace 'relevant' with explicit selection criteria; replace 'appropriate template' with a concrete decision table; define 'recent' as a time window

evidence: 'appropriate location' step 4; 'relevant resources' step 8; 'relevant pages' appears 6+ times
description: Two or more distinct vague quantifiers with 'relevant' appearing 6+ times; penalty capped at -20
suggested_fix: Replace 'relevant' with explicit criteria (e.g., 'pages linked from the spec page or sharing a tag with the spec'); replace 'appropriate location' with a rule
